label: Localized molluscum contagiosum without genital or immunocompromised-host features
inclusion_criteria: Adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of localized molluscum contagiosum.
No genital, eye, extensive, secondary infection, immunocompromised-host, pediatric, pregnancy, or specialist-directed pathway.
exclusion_criteria: Genital or sexually transmitted infection pathway, eye involvement, extensive or severe disease, immunocompromised host, or secondary infection.
Pediatric pathway, pregnancy, poor follow-up, or specialist-directed dermatology plan.
Uncertain rash diagnosis needing biopsy, STI testing, or another pathway.
must_not_miss: Rash mimic requiring biopsy or urgent dermatology.
default_follow_up: Follow up with primary care or dermatology if bumps are spreading, bothersome, infected-appearing, or not improving as expected.

source_audit.notes: MedlinePlus and CDC support molluscum contagiosum framing, raised pearl-like viral papules, usual mild self-limited course, contagious spread by direct contact or contaminated objects, and avoiding scratching or shaving over lesions. Autonomous Phase 1601-1650 approved this narrow v1 only for clinician-diagnosed localized molluscum without genital, eye, extensive, secondary infection, immunocompromised-host, pediatric, pregnancy, poor-follow-up, or specialist-directed pathway.

review.notes: Reviewed as a narrow production ontology phenotype. required_modifiers: adult with clinician diagnosis or discharge impression of localized molluscum contagiosum
no genital involvement, eye involvement, secondary infection, or immunocompromised-host context documented
no pediatric, pregnancy, extensive, poor-follow-up, or specialist-directed pathway
